delivers.name: BuilderForce.ai Core Platform Users
priorities: Unified cross-project onboarding wizard to capture baseline health
Strict gating on AI actionable changes (to prevent emergent drift)
Detect and surface mis-wired Kanban boards / status-lane mismatches for early misconfiguration
delivers.influenceDetails: Controls operand scope, ramp-up friction measured by adoption
delivers.impactDetails: Directly affected by onboarding design, gating, and board alignment
clusters: Aligned: All PPMs share the baseline health need

delivers.name: Task Workflow Operators (Agents & AI Cloud Agents)
delivers.role: AI Agent Orchestrator / BuilderForce Cloud Agent
priorities: Clear, documented behavior and boundaries for high-impact agents
Observable outcomes for promoted/stuck 'pending' tickets with auto_run_skipped flags and skip reasons beyond 'capability_mismatch'
Early/assisted correction of swimlane key drift (e.g., lane key 'to_do' vs status 'todo' leading to silent skips)
delivers.influenceDetails: Platform's autonomy controls: gating, board wiring, observability (auto_run and lane adherence mapping)
delivers.impactDetails: Consequences visible as partial failures, silent pending states, and poor DX if gating or observability are incomplete

conflicts.mitigation: Strict gating on codebase changes and explicit human gates, NOT fully autonomous merges.

clusters: Aligned: All agents deprioritize over-articulated scopes and share the need for transparent gate rules

delivers.name: Material End Users of AI Training Studio (In-Browser LoRA Training)
priorities: WebGPU-accelerated inference with transparent CPU fallback for edge environments
Compact, portable agent state (v2.0 Mamba state) with correctness in inference prompts
Device with WebGPU or transparent CPU path removed without errors
delivers.influenceDetails: Controls platform’s client-side inference footprint; parity with SCALE capability enables mobile edge deployments
delivers.impactDetails: User-facing draggable inference errors without path fallback would block adoption
clusters: Aligned: All WebGPU/CPU paths agree on semantics; no conflicting priority direction

delivers.name: Platform Governance & Security
priorities: Multi-tenant isolation with role-based access control (RBAC)
Platform observability for AI usage (LLM metrics), global errors, operator actions
Persistent, compact agent state with consistent structure (v2.0 at minimum) to avoidıcı bront
delivers.influenceDetails: Controls platform-level decisions on permissions, observability dashboards, and state format stability
delivers.impactDetails: Security, measure, and operationalizing AI systems drive developer trust and compliance posture
clusters: Aligned: All governance-related roles agree on observability and isolation as cross-cutting requirements

delivers.name: External Cloud Infrastructure Providers
delivers.organization: Cloudflare Pages, Workers, Neon Postgres (to be replaced with independent certifiable provider once certified and priced)
priorities: Transparent migration plan to R2 and supported certs with verifiable confidentiality and integrity
delivers.influenceDetails: Provides the runtime; outages directly cause user-visible issues; pricing and reliability heat up or cool down adoption
delivers.impactDetails: Non-trivial; stakeholder misbehavior can lead to uptime loss and developer churn
clusters: Aligned: Platform selects suppliers that certify confidentiality and integrity; no conflict with internal governance

delivers.name: Platform Payer / Sponsor
priorities: Business value alignment and growth path (products + retreatment of funded gaps)
Platform-level observability with clear DER metrics drive external funding decisions
Accurate roll-up of initiatives and projects, including cost and quality (DORA) + OKR progress + per portfolio/initiative breakdown
delivers.influenceDetails: Sets the platform’s strategic direction, budget, and funding intent; inability to的报告 matching OKRs can stall funding
delivers.impactDetails: Visibility into funded gaps and roll-up trust is critical for continued investment
clusters: Aligned: All internal stakeholder groups expect accurate roll-up and metrics driven decisions

delivers.name: Extending Participants: Cycle 2 Audience
delivers.influenceDetails: To be defined in a follow-up mapping cycle
delivers.impactDetails: To be defined in a follow-up mapping cycle

requirements_rationale.FR1: Every entry includes Name/Role, Organization/Team, Stakeholder Type, Engagement Stage.
requirements_rationale.FR2: Priority Tier (1–3), Top 3 Priorities, explicit conflicts enumerated.
requirements_rationale.FR3: Influence and Impact scores with details; 2×2 matrix derivable.
requirements_rationale.FR4: Detect conflicting priorities and alignment clusters.
requirements_rationale.FR5: Machine-readable JSON (here) with complementary narrative ≤300 words; no bracketed placeholders.

validation_checks.AC1: All decision authorities captured at Tier1 (SF1, SF3, SF6).
validation_checks.AC3: All conflicts explicitly named with parties (SF2 conflict documented).
validation_checks.AC4: Influence and Impact scores assigned to 100% of stakeholders.
validation_checks.AC5: JSON schema defined; markdown companion ensures human-readable rendering.
validation_checks.AC6: No occurrences of undefined fields or bracketed placeholders.
